perturbation.original_text: on behalf of the Donor (‘the Purpose’) the Recipient undertakes that it will respect and preserve the confidentiality of the Information and it will not without the ex press prior consent of the Donor either:
perturbation.changed_text: on behalf of the Donor (‘the Purpose’) the Recipient warrants that it will respect and preserve the secrecy of the Information and it will not without the ex press prior consent of the Owner either:
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'undertakes' with 'warrants' and 'Donor' with 'Owner'. Changing 'undertakes' to 'warrants' elevates the obligation's strength, possibly introducing unintended legal consequences. Replacing 'Donor' with 'Owner' creates confusion, especially when 'Donor' is consistently used elsewhere. This contradiction affects the clarity and enforceability of the confidentiality obligations and the 'Owner' term isn't present anywhere else in the text, making it completely ambigous which affects the overall obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: communicate or otherwise make available the Information to any third party, other than: a) an employee of the Recipient who requires the Information in connection with his employment and then only if the employee is bound by conditions of secrecy no less strict that those set out in this Agreement which conditions Recipient hereby agrees to enforce at the request of the Donor ; and/or b) the UK Intellectual Property Office, the European Patent Office, the United States Patent and Trademark Office, the World Intellectual Property Organisation, and any other national or international intellectual property office worldwide , but only with respect to the Applications in accordance with the Purpose ; and/or c) any person in any jur isdiction worldwide who has been authorised by the Donor or Recipient to act on behalf of, or otherwise assist, the Donor with respect to one or more of the Applications , and any duly authorised legal representative of the Donor in any jurisdiction worldwi de; and/or
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Donor' to 'Owner' introduces a misaligned terminology issue, which as per the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, leads to in-text contradictions because 'Owner' isn't consistently used elsewhere in the document, creating ambiguity. The contradicted text references the 'Donor' multiple times, particularly in relation to authorizations and requests, which directly conflicts with the introduction of a new, undefined 'Owner'. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency within the document regarding who has the authority to enforce or request certain actions, and breaks the compatibility between the Changed Text and Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: 1.1 communicate or otherwise make available the Information to any third party, other than: a) an employee of the Recipient who requires the Information in connection with his employment and then only if the employee is bound by conditions of secrecy no less strict that those set out in this Agreement which conditions Recipient hereby agrees to enforce at the request of the Donor ; and/or b) the UK Intellectual Property Office, the European Patent Office, the United States Patent and Trademark Office, the World Intellectual Property Organisation, and any other national or international intellectual property office worldwide , but only with respect to the Applications in accordance with the Purpose ; and/or c) any person in any jur isdiction worldwide who has been authorised by the Donor or Recipient to act on behalf of, or otherwise assist, the Donor with respect to one or more of the Applications , and any duly authorised legal representative of the Donor in any jurisdiction worldwi de; and/or
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 disclose or otherwise share the Data with any third party, except: a) an employee of the Recipient who requires the Data in connection with his employment and then only if the employee is bound by conditions of secrecy no less strict that those set out in this Agreement which conditions Recipient hereby agrees to enforce at the request of the Donor ; and/or b) the UK Intellectual Property Office, the European Patent Office, the United States Patent and Trademark Office, the World Intellectual Property Organisation, and any other national or international intellectual property office worldwide , but only with respect to the Applications in accordance with the Purpose ; and/or c) any person in any jur isdiction worldwide who has been authorised by the Donor or Recipient to act on behalf of, or otherwise assist, the Donor with respect to one or more of the Applications , and any duly authorised legal representative of the Donor in any jurisdiction worldwi de; and/or
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'communicate or otherwise make available the Information' with 'disclose or otherwise share the Data'. 'Information' is replaced with 'Data'. This can cause uncertainty if the agreement does not clearly define that 'Information' and 'Data' are the same thing. This ambiguity will impact how this clause is interpreted, because it will not be known whether the term 'Data' refers to a subset, superset, or precisely the same set of information which the rest of the document reffers to as 'Information'. The use of the term 'Information' creates an ambiguity since the document started by reffering to the information that will be disclosed as 'Information', making the term 'Data' a contradiction within the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the disclosure to it by the Donor of information (whether or not contained in documents) relating to a _______________________ (‘the Information’) for the purposes of the preparation, filing and prosecution of one or more patent applications (‘the Applications’) on behalf of the Donor (‘the Purpose’) the Recipient undertakes that it will respect and preserve the confidentiality of the Information and it will not without the ex press prior consent of the Donor either: 1.1 communicate or otherwise make available the Information to any third party, other than: a) an employee of the Recipient who requires the Information in connection with his employment and then only if the employee is bound by conditions of secrecy no less strict that those set out in this Agreement which conditions Recipient hereby agrees to enforce at the request of the Donor ; and/or b) the UK Intellectual Property Office, the European Patent Office, the United States Patent and Trademark Office, the World Intellectual Property Organisation, and any other national or international intellectual property office worldwide , but only with respect to the Applications in accordance with the Purpose ; and/or c) any person in any jur isdiction worldwide who has been authorised by the Donor or Recipient to act on behalf of, or otherwise assist, the Donor with respect to one or more of the Applications , and any duly authorised legal representative of the Donor in any jurisdiction worldwi de; and/or 1.2 use the Information for any investigation, research, development or manufacture, other than so far as any such activity is essential for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text both refer to 'the Information'. The changed text replaces 'Information' with 'Data'. Because the agreement does not explicitly state 'Information' and 'Data' are synonymous, this creates ambiguity and a direct contradiction, as it is no longer clear whether the disclosure restrictions apply to both 'Information' and 'Data' or just 'Data'. The contradicted text specifically refers to restrictions on communicating 'the Information', whereas the changed text restricts disclosing 'the Data'. This leads to uncertainty, making the obligations inconsistent and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.2 use the Information for any investigation, research, development or manufacture, other than so far as any such activity is essential for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 utilise the Details for any study, research, development or making, other than so far as any such activity is vital for the reason.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'use the Information' with 'utilise the Details' and 'manufacture' with 'making' and 'essential' with 'vital' and 'Purpose' with 'reason'. This use of different terms for similar concepts introduces ambiguity. It's unclear if 'Information' is the same as 'Details', 'manufacture' is the same as 'making', 'essential' the same as 'vital' and 'Purpose' the same as 'reason'. This affects the scope of permitted activities under this clause, as it is not defined whether the same standards apply to 'Details' than to 'Information' and all the other terms. All of this leads to potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the disclosure to it by the Donor of information (whether or not contained in documents) relating to a _______________________ (‘the Information’) for the purposes of the preparation, filing and prosecution of one or more patent applications (‘the Applications’) on behalf of the Donor (‘the Purpose’) the Recipient undertakes that it will respect and preserve the confidentiality of the Information and it will not without the ex press prior consent of the Donor either: 1.1 communicate or otherwise make available the Information to any third party, other than: a) an employee of the Recipient who requires the Information in connection with his employment and then only if the employee is bound by conditions of secrecy no less strict that those set out in this Agreement which conditions Recipient hereby agrees to enforce at the request of the Donor ; and/or b) the UK Intellectual Property Office, the European Patent Office, the United States Patent and Trademark Office, the World Intellectual Property Organisation, and any other national or international intellectual property office worldwide , but only with respect to the Applications in accordance with the Purpose ; and/or c) any person in any jur isdiction worldwide who has been authorised by the Donor or Recipient to act on behalf of, or otherwise assist, the Donor with respect to one or more of the Applications , and any duly authorised legal representative of the Donor in any jurisdiction worldwi de; and/or 1.2 use the Information for any investigation, research, development or manufacture, other than so far as any such activity is essential for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity by replacing 'Information' with 'Details', 'manufacture' with 'making', 'essential' with 'vital' and 'Purpose' with 'reason'. The contradicted text defines 'the Information' and 'the Purpose'. The changes introduce misaligned terminology. The original text (1.2) uses 'Information' and 'Purpose', mirroring the definitions. By changing these key terms to 'Details' and 'reason', without clarifying whether 'Details' is interchangeable with 'Information' or 'reason' with 'Purpose', a conflict arises, leading to a contradiction. This falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology leading to in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3 The above obligations shall also apply to any sample or article incorporating or derived from the Information and whether or not provided by the Donor (‘Samples’).
perturbation.changed_text: 3 The above duties shall also apply to any sample or item integrating or resulting from the Information and whether or not furnished by the Donor (‘Samples’).
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'obligations' with 'duties', 'article' with 'item', 'incorporating' with 'integrating', and 'provided' with 'furnished'. This creates an inconsistency within the contract. The terms 'duties' and 'obligations' may imply different legal weight. The 'article' and 'item' can also mean different things, creating a contradiction that can alter the responsibility of the involved parties. All of this affects whether the 'Samples' should be treated differently.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The above obligations shall not apply or shall cease to apply to such of the Information as the Recipient can show to the reasonable satisfaction of the Donor: 2.1 has become public knowledge other than through the fault of the Recipient; or 2.2 was already known to the Recipient prior to disclosure by the Donor; or 2.3 has been received from a third party who did not acquire it in confidence from the Donor or from someone owing a duty of confidence to the Donor.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation replaces key terms like 'obligations' with 'duties,' 'article' with 'item,' 'incorporating' with 'integrating,' and 'provided' with 'furnished.' This creates misaligned terminology, as the terms are used interchangeably but not defined. The explanation states 'duties' and 'obligations' may imply different legal weight. The 'article' and 'item' can also mean different things, creating a contradiction that can alter the responsibility of the involved parties. The contradicted text references specific 'obligations', and if these obligations are changed to 'duties' with potentially different legal meanings, it creates an in-text contradiction, affecting how the 'Samples' are treated and making enforcement unclear under the definition of Misaligned Terminology.
